scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Point to the menu if needed and keep the order short when the shop is busy.

phrases.context: Use this for a standard iced coffee order.

phrases.english: I would like an iced black coffee
phrases.context: Use this when you want coffee without milk.

phrases.english: I would like an iced latte
phrases.context: Useful when ordering a familiar milk coffee drink.
Add this when you want less ice in your drink.
Use this to keep your drink less sweet.
Say this when you want takeaway instead of staying.
Use this when you are ready to pay or ask for the bill.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Pointing helps, but simple phrases are useful for spice level, water, and takeaway.

phrases.context: Use this for a simple food order while pointing.
Use this while pointing at the dish you want.
Say this before the food is prepared if you want mild food.
Use this when you want extra greens or garnish.
Ask this when you want bottled water with your meal.
Use this if utensils were not set out.
Say this when you want takeaway from a stall or restaurant.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Showing the destination on your phone helps even when the language is limited.

phrases.context: Use this while showing your destination on your phone.
Use this for a simple destination when the driver already knows the area.
Use this when you want the driver to stop at the current spot.
Use this if you need to guide the driver.

phrases.english: Please turn on the air conditioning
phrases.context: Use this on a hot ride if the air conditioning is off.
Use this if you need a very short stop.
Use this when payment method needs to be clear.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Ask the price before agreeing and keep small bills ready when shopping.

phrases.context: Use this while pointing at an item.
Use this for produce or anything sold by weight.
Say this lightly when reacting to a high price.

phrases.english: Can you lower it a little
phrases.context: Use this politely when bargaining is normal.
Ask this before making a final decision.
Use this when you want to compare options.
Say this once you agree on the item and price.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: A friendly tone and a simple greeting go a long way in travel situations.

phrases.context: Use this to greet people in casual travel moments.
Say this anytime someone helps you.
Use this as a respectful yes or acknowledgment.
Use this to politely decline something.
Use this to get attention politely or open a short request.
Say this when someone apologizes to you.
Use this when leaving a shop, hotel, or ride.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Mini-marts are useful for water, tissues, medicine, and quick snacks during a long day out.

phrases.context: Use this for a quick water purchase.
Ask this if you need a carry bag.
Use this when you need tissues at the counter.
Ask this when you need help finding sunscreen.
Use this for bottled drinks or hard packaging.
Ask this before checkout if you are short on cash.
Use this if you need the receipt for your purchase.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Keep your passport and booking details ready because front desks often need them at check-in.

phrases.context: Use this first when you arrive at reception.

phrases.english: I would like to check in
phrases.context: Say this when you are ready to start check-in.
Ask this early so you know the departure time.
Use this if the room feels too warm.

phrases.english: The air conditioner is not working
phrases.context: Say this when you need staff to check the AC.
Use this when you need extra towels.
Use this if you need the desk to hold your bags.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Save your hotel name and one landmark in your phone so you can ask for help even if maps fail.

phrases.english: Excuse me how do I get to the city center
phrases.context: Use this when asking for a route to a known area or landmark.
Ask this when deciding whether to walk or ride.
Use this to check whether walking is realistic.
Ask this when someone is giving step by step directions.
Use this to confirm the next turn.
Use this to confirm you should keep moving forward.
Use this to politely end the directions exchange.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: If something goes wrong, stay calm and show booking screens, photos, or screenshots to speed up help.

phrases.context: Use this when you need immediate help finding your way.
Say this when someone is speaking too quickly.

phrases.english: Can you speak a little slower
phrases.context: Use this when you need the other person to slow down.
Say this if you forgot an item in a car, room, or cafe.

phrases.english: The Wi Fi is not working
phrases.context: Use this at hotels or cafes when you cannot connect.
Use this if you feel sick and need medical help.
Say this when you need someone to make a call for you.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Short friendly chat often starts with where you are from, whether it is your first visit, and what food you like.

phrases.english: I am from the United States
phrases.context: Use this when someone asks where you are from.
Use this as a simple answer when introducing yourself.
Say this when talking about your first visit.
Use this as an easy positive comment in conversation.
Use this to compliment a meal or recommendation.
Use this as easy weather small talk on a hot day.
Use this with friendly staff or locals after a greeting.
